report_name: Pennington County Government Shuts Down Most Offices Citing "Cybersecurity Incident"

identities.name: Pennington County
identities.description: A county government in South Dakota that shut down most public offices following a significant cybersecurity incident in July 2026.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 7b2d5d4a-1a3b-4c6e-8f9a-9b8c7d6e5f4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.kotaradio.com/2026/07/06/pennington-county-closes-most-public-offices-following-cybersecurity-incident/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
modified: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects command-line patterns for vssadmin deleting volume shadow copies, a technique often used by ransomware to prevent system recovery.

indicators.name: Potential Brute Force or Password Spray Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Brute Force or Password Spray Attempt
id: 8c3e6e5b-2b4c-4d7f-9a0b-0c9d8e7f6a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows logon failures (Event ID 4625). A high volume of these events can indicate a brute-force or password spray attack against network services.
references:
  - https://www.kotaradio.com/2026/07/06/pennington-county-closes-most-public-offices-following-cybersecurity-incident/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
modified: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1110
  - attack.credential_access
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4625
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users entering incorrect passwords.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects Windows logon failures (Event ID 4625). A high volume of these events suggests a potential brute-force or password spray attack.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: 9d4f7f6c-3c5d-4e8a-0b1c-1d0e9f8a7b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of encoded commands in PowerShell, a common technique used by attackers to obfuscate malicious scripts.
references:
  - https://www.kotaradio.com/2026/07/06/pennington-county-closes-most-public-offices-following-cybersecurity-incident/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
modified: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Some legitimate scripts and administration tools may use encoded commands.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, a common obfuscation technique for malicious scripts.
